# tick 6 — 2026-06-20 22:43 PT

Cake (cron) shipped 5 features on `feature/autoship`. All five
selected from the open backlog. Gates green; pushed in a single
batch after the last commit.

## Features

1. **`da07739` feat(extract/receipt): detect inclusive vs exclusive tax mode**
   - New `ReceiptFields.tax_mode` (str | None) with values
     `"inclusive"` / `"exclusive"` / `None`.
   - Inclusive vocab covers EU / AU / NZ / IN phrasings: `VAT
     included`, `incl. VAT`, `tax included`, `incl. GST`, `GST
     inclusive`, `all prices include tax`, `inclusive of HST`.
   - Exclusive vocab covers US sales-tax + UK shorthand: `+ tax`,
     `plus tax`, `tax extra`, `tax not included`, `excl. VAT`,
     `ex GST`, `prices exclude tax`, `exclusive of GST`.
   - Tiebreaker when both appear: first cue in OCR order wins
     (matches how a human reads top-to-bottom).
   - Wired into `parse_receipt_text` + `enrich_receipt` (caller-
     supplied value preserved) + LLM wire format in
     `classify/client.py`.
   - 35 new tests.

2. **`f06edea` feat(extract/receipt): detect party size and split-bill count**
   - New `ReceiptFields.party_size` (int | None) for restaurant
     cover counts.
   - Recognises: `Party of 4`, `Party Size: 6`, `Party 2`,
     `Guests: 3`, `Guest count: 5`, `# of Guests 4`, `Covers: 8`,
     `Split 3 ways`, `Split between 4`, `Per person (4)`.
   - Cover-count terms (Party / Guests / Covers) win over split
     cues (Split N ways) because cover count is the source of
     truth (a 4-cover bill split 3 ways still has 4 guests).
   - Counts bounded 1..50 to reject OCR noise; three-digit fails
     the regex digit cap as a second defence; bare `Party N`
     requires a leading colon / comma / line-start so prose
     ("the party 3 days ago") does not fire.
   - LLM wire format updated.
   - 39 new tests.

3. **`2460910` feat(extract/code): TypeScript-specific feature extraction**
   - New `CodeFields.ts_features` (list[str]) surfacing
     TS-only constructs beyond the existing minimum-language
     hints. Tags: `decorator`, `as_cast`, `angle_cast`, `generic`,
     `enum`, `readonly`, `abstract`, `access_modifier`,
     `namespace`, `optional_chain`, `non_null_assert`.
   - Detection runs ONLY when the language layer already tagged
     the snippet as typescript / tsx / ts so a plain JS snippet
     using `as` as a variable name does not false-positive.
   - Decorator regex requires leading uppercase identifier to
     skip template-literal `@` usages.
   - Optional-chain regex anchored away from ternaries.
   - Non-null-assert regex anchored away from `!foo` (logical
     not) and `foo !== bar` (strict inequality).
   - One regex tighten during dev: `access_modifier` was
     start-of-line anchored on the first pass and missed inline
     `class Foo { private id: number; }` declarations; relaxed
     to word-boundary on the left with a trailing-token
     constraint (`[:(?=]`) to keep prose words from misfiring.
     Folded into the commit before push.
   - LLM wire format updated.
   - 47 new tests.

4. **`a0ccc41` feat(extract/code): detect minified vs hand-written JS**
   - New `CodeFields.minified` (bool).
   - Known bundler preambles short-circuit to True:
     `(self.webpackChunk`, `webpackBootstrap`, `webpackJsonp`,
     `function(modules)`, `!function(`, `var __webpack_modules__`,
     `globalThis.webpackChunk`, `// minified`.
   - Heuristic: avg non-empty line length > 250 chars OR any
     line > 500 chars, AND < 30% of `;` / `{` / `}` separators
     followed by a newline. Dual gate keeps long-template-literal
     snippets in pretty code from false-positiving.
   - Detection restricted to JS-family languages (javascript /
     typescript / jsx / tsx). Other languages return False
     unconditionally because the heuristic is tuned to JS
     bundle output.
   - LLM wire format updated.
   - 22 new tests.

5. **`49a28b6` feat(extract/error): Python SyntaxError caret-line extraction**
   - `ErrorFields.message` enriched with the offending source
     line and caret column span when CPython prints the
     `^^^^^^^` indicator. CPython 3.10+ widened `~~~~^^^^~~~~`
     shapes (tildes cover the expression, carets pin the
     operator) are also captured -- full span goes into the
     message.
   - Six `likely_cause` hints: invalid syntax, unexpected EOF,
     unmatched bracket, missing trailing `:` (def / if / for /
     class), f-string syntax error, indentation/tab mix.
   - Exception name overridden from the trailing line: one of
     `SyntaxError`, `IndentationError`, `TabError`,
     `UnicodeDecodeError`, `UnicodeEncodeError`.
   - New helper `parse_syntax_caret(text) -> (exc, source,
     start, end)` exported for direct dashboard use.
   - Defensive matching rejects malformed dividers (a line
     containing `^` plus non-caret/tilde chars), skips caret
     lines at index 0 (no source above), and skips caret lines
     whose source line is blank.
   - 21 new tests.

## Risks / notes (new this tick)

- Receipt `tax_mode` tiebreaker is "first cue in OCR order wins".
  The helper checks the inclusive vocab first internally only as
  a deterministic implementation choice; the actual decision is
  based on the smallest start offset.
- TS `access_modifier` regex is intentionally relaxed (no
  start-of-line anchor) so inline class declarations tag
  correctly. The trailing `[:(?=]` constraint keeps prose words
  after `private` from misfiring.
- The Python SyntaxError enrichment lives INSIDE the existing
  Python branch of `parse_error_text` -- it does NOT add a new
  elif. The caret-tail is appended only when `parse_syntax_caret`
  returns a hit, so a SyntaxError without the caret still tags
  as python with just the bare message.
- `CodeFields.minified` returns False unconditionally for non-JS
  languages even when their line stats look minified, because
  the heuristic is JS-bundle-specific. A future ticket can add a
  Python-aware minified detector (one-liner Python golf scripts).
